response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer but lacks the core C++ expertise central to this role. C++ appears only briefly in early-career work at National Instruments (2000-2001) and a bachelor thesis project; recent decades focus on TypeScript, Node.js, D, and web platforms. No demonstrated experience with IEC 62443, SDL, industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), MQTT, or message brokers. The resume also contains implausible date overlaps and inconsistencies (e.g., employment dates predating stated education and birth-plausible timelines), which raises credibility concerns.
strengths: Long, broad software engineering career across web, mobile, embedded, and distributed systems
Some exposure to low-latency, high-throughput systems and asynchronous I/O (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong testing culture and mentoring background (TDD, workshops, code katas)
concerns: Minimal recent C++/STL experience; primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/D
No evidence of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity work
Missing required domain protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA) and industrial automation background; resume timeline inconsistencies

key_factors.factor: No IEC 62443 / SDL / industrial security experience
Broad senior engineering and mentoring background
